contents.value:
```ssh-config
Host myserver
    HostName 10.0.1.50
    User deploy

Host *.internal
    ProxyJump bastion

Host *
    ServerAliveInterval 60
```
**Host** blocks match by name/pattern. `Host *` sets defaults. Wildcards and negation (`!pattern`) supported.

```ssh-config
Host myserver
    HostName 192.168.1.100

Host staging
    HostName staging.example.com
```
**HostName** specifies the real hostname or IP to connect to. Allows the `Host` alias to differ from the actual address.

```ssh-config
Host production
    User deploy

Host *
    User myusername
```
**User** sets the login username. Overrides the default (current local user) for specific hosts.

```ssh-config
Host myserver
    Port 2222

Host *
    Port 22
```
**Port** specifies the remote SSH port. Default is 22. Some servers use non-standard ports for security.

```ssh-config
IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
IdentityFile ~/.ssh/work_key
```
**IdentityFile** specifies the private key file. Multiple keys can be listed — SSH tries them in order.

```ssh-config
Host work
    IdentityFile ~/.ssh/work_key
    IdentitiesOnly yes
```
**IdentitiesOnly** restricts authentication to the specified IdentityFile(s) only, ignoring keys from ssh-agent.

```ssh-config
Host internal
    HostName 10.0.1.50
    ProxyJump bastion

# Chaining: ProxyJump host1,host2
```
**ProxyJump** connects through a bastion/jump host. Supports chaining multiple jumps.

```ssh-config
Host internal
    ProxyCommand ssh -W %h:%p bastion

Host corporate
    ProxyCommand nc -x proxy:1080 %h %p
```
**ProxyCommand** specifies a command to connect to the server. `%h` and `%p` are replaced with host and port.

```ssh-config
LocalForward 5432 db-server:5432
LocalForward 8080 localhost:80
```
**LocalForward** tunnels a local port to a remote destination via SSH. Great for accessing internal databases.

```ssh-config
RemoteForward 8080 localhost:3000
```
**RemoteForward** makes a local service accessible from the remote host. The remote port forwards back to local.

```ssh-config
DynamicForward 1080
```
**DynamicForward** creates a SOCKS proxy on the specified local port. All traffic through the proxy is tunneled over SSH.

```ssh-config
ForwardAgent yes
# Security: only enable for trusted hosts
```
**ForwardAgent** forwards your SSH agent to the remote host. Useful for multi-hop but risky on untrusted servers.

```ssh-config
Host *
    ServerAliveInterval 60
    ServerAliveCountMax 3
```
**ServerAliveInterval** sends keep-alive packets every N seconds. Prevents idle disconnections.

```ssh-config
ServerAliveCountMax 3
```
**ServerAliveCountMax** sets how many keep-alive messages can go unanswered before disconnecting. With interval 60 and max 3, timeout is 180s.

```ssh-config
Host *
    AddKeysToAgent yes
```
**AddKeysToAgent** automatically adds keys to the running ssh-agent after first use. Values: `yes`, `no`, `ask`, `confirm`, or a time limit.

```ssh-config
Host *
    StrictHostKeyChecking ask

Host *.test
    StrictHostKeyChecking no
    UserKnownHostsFile /dev/null
```
**StrictHostKeyChecking** controls host key verification. `ask` (default) prompts, `yes` rejects unknown, `no` accepts all (insecure).

```ssh-config
UserKnownHostsFile ~/.ssh/known_hosts
UserKnownHostsFile /dev/null
```
**UserKnownHostsFile** specifies the file for storing known host keys. Use `/dev/null` to disable (with `StrictHostKeyChecking no`).

```ssh-config
Host slow-link
    Compression yes
```
**Compression** enables zlib compression. Useful for slow connections but can increase latency on fast ones.

```ssh-config
TCPKeepAlive yes
```
**TCPKeepAlive** sends TCP keepalive messages to detect dead connections. Different from `ServerAliveInterval` (application-level).

```ssh-config
Host *
    ControlMaster auto
    ControlPath ~/.ssh/sockets/%r@%h-%p
    ControlPersist 600
```
**ControlMaster** enables connection multiplexing. Subsequent connections to the same host reuse the existing TCP connection.

```ssh-config
ControlPath ~/.ssh/sockets/%r@%h-%p
```
**ControlPath** specifies the Unix socket file for connection multiplexing. `%r` = user, `%h` = host, `%p` = port.

```ssh-config
ControlPersist 600
ControlPersist yes
```
**ControlPersist** keeps the master connection open after the last session closes. Value in seconds, or `yes` for indefinite.

```ssh-config
Match host *.internal user deploy
    ProxyJump bastion

Match exec "test -f ~/.ssh/work_key"
    IdentityFile ~/.ssh/work_key
```
**Match** applies settings conditionally by host, user, or command result. More flexible than `Host`.

```ssh-config
Include ~/.ssh/config.d/*
Include ~/.ssh/work_config
```
**Include** imports other config files. Supports glob patterns. Useful for splitting configs by project or team.

```ssh-config
Host problematic
    LogLevel DEBUG3

Host *
    LogLevel ERROR
```
**LogLevel** controls SSH client logging verbosity. Levels: `QUIET`, `FATAL`, `ERROR`, `INFO`, `VERBOSE`, `DEBUG`, `DEBUG1`-`DEBUG3`.

```ssh-config
Host scripts
    RequestTTY no

Host interactive
    RequestTTY force
```
**RequestTTY** controls pseudo-terminal allocation. `no` for scripts, `force` for interactive. Equivalent to `-t`/`-T` flags.

```ssh-config
Host automated
    BatchMode yes
```
**BatchMode** disables password prompting and host key confirmation. Useful for scripts where no user interaction is possible.

```ssh-config
Host *
    ConnectTimeout 10
```
**ConnectTimeout** sets the connection timeout in seconds. Default uses the system TCP timeout.

```ssh-config
Host unreliable
    ConnectionAttempts 3
```
**ConnectionAttempts** specifies the number of connection retry attempts before giving up. Default is 1.

```ssh-config
Host *
    Ciphers aes256-gcm@openssh.com,chacha20-poly1305@openssh.com
```
**Ciphers** specifies allowed encryption algorithms in preference order.

```ssh-config
KexAlgorithms curve25519-sha256,diffie-hellman-group16-sha512
```
**KexAlgorithms** specifies allowed key exchange algorithms. Modern setups prefer curve25519.

```ssh-config
Host *
    PubkeyAuthentication yes
    PasswordAuthentication no
```
**PubkeyAuthentication** enables/disables public key authentication. Best practice: enable keys, disable passwords.

```ssh-config
Host legacy-server
    PasswordAuthentication yes

Host *
    PasswordAuthentication no
```
**PasswordAuthentication** controls password-based login. Disable for security when key auth is available.

```ssh-config
Host desktop
    ForwardX11 yes
    ForwardX11Trusted yes
```
**ForwardX11** enables X11 forwarding for running graphical applications remotely. `Trusted` bypasses X11 security.

```ssh-config
Host *
    SendEnv LANG LC_*
    SetEnv MY_VAR=value
```
**SendEnv** sends local environment variables to the remote server. Server must accept them via `AcceptEnv`.

```ssh-config
Host staging
    SetEnv ENV=staging
    SetEnv DEBUG=1
```
**SetEnv** sets environment variables on the remote session. Does not require server-side `AcceptEnv` configuration.

```ssh-config
Host *
    PermitLocalCommand yes
    LocalCommand echo "Connected to %h"
```
**PermitLocalCommand** allows running local commands after connecting. `LocalCommand` specifies the command.

```ssh-config
CanonicalizeHostname yes
CanonicalDomains example.com internal.example.com
```
**CanonicalizeHostname** resolves short hostnames to FQDNs using `CanonicalDomains`. Useful in corporate networks.

```ssh-config
Host *
    AddressFamily inet
```
**AddressFamily** restricts connections to `inet` (IPv4), `inet6` (IPv6), or `any` (default). Use `inet` to avoid IPv6 issues.

```ssh-config
Host *
    PreferredAuthentications publickey,keyboard-interactive,password
```
**PreferredAuthentications** sets the order of authentication methods to try.

```ssh-config
Host *.corp.example.com
    GSSAPIAuthentication yes
    GSSAPIDelegateCredentials yes

Host *
    GSSAPIAuthentication no
```
**GSSAPIAuthentication** enables Kerberos/GSSAPI authentication. Common in corporate environments with Active Directory.

```ssh-config
String
```
Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

```ssh-config
Number
```
Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

```ssh-config
Boolean
```
Logical type with two values: true and false.

```ssh-config
Array
```
Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

```ssh-config
Object
```
Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

```ssh-config
null
```
Represents the intentional absence of any value.

```ssh-config
undefined
```
Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

```ssh-config
print(value)
```
Output a value to the standard output stream.
